meta_title: Impact Windows in Fort Lauderdale, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Fort Lauderdale, FL. HVHZ-compliant, 170mph rated. Save on insurance. Free quotes — call today!
h1: Fort Lauderdale's Trusted Impact Window Experts — Built for the HVHZ
intro:
Fort Lauderdale is one of the most beautiful cities in South Florida — and one of the most weather-exposed. Nestled along Broward County's coastline just one mile from the Atlantic, neighborhoods like Las Olas, Victoria Park, Rio Vista, and Sailboat Bend face a relentless combination of salt air, summer storm surges, and the very real threat of catastrophic hurricane-force winds. With a designated wind zone rating of 170 mph and full inclusion in Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), Fort Lauderdale carries some of the strictest building code requirements in the entire country — and for good reason. Every home here deserves windows that can meet that standard.

For the more than 50% of Fort Lauderdale homes built before 1994 — many of which still feature single-pane aluminum windows that predate modern hurricane codes — the risk is especially significant. These older homes in Tarpon River, Flagler Village, and throughout the city's historic corridors were constructed before the hard lessons of Hurricane Andrew reshaped Florida's approach to residential construction. A house valued at $480,000, the average in Fort Lauderdale, deserves the kind of protection that reflects its worth. Impact windows aren't just a smart upgrade for these homes — they're a critical line of defense.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've made it our mission to protect South Florida homes with the highest-quality impact window solutions on the market. As 'The Kings of Service,' we bring 60+ years of combined installation experience, 100% in-house crews, and deep familiarity with Broward County's permitting and code landscape to every project we take on in Fort Lauderdale. Whether you live steps from Fort Lauderdale Beach, along the Riverwalk, or in a quiet residential pocket near Oakland Park or Wilton Manors, we have the expertise and the products to give your home the protection it needs — and the peace of mind you deserve.

section_why.h2: Why Fort Lauderdale Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When it comes to impact window installation in Fort Lauderdale, not all contractors are created equal. The HVHZ designation that covers all of Broward County means your windows must meet an exceptionally demanding set of engineering and installation standards — and the difference between a properly installed, code-compliant impact window and a corner-cut job can mean the difference between a home that survives a major hurricane and one that doesn't. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing was built from the ground up to meet that challenge head-on.

Unlike many competitors who rely on subcontracted labor, every single installation we perform is completed by our own trained, in-house crew. This matters enormously in a city like Fort Lauderdale, where building inspectors at the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division are thorough, permit requirements are precise, and HVHZ compliance leaves zero margin for error. Our team has completed 500+ homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, giving us an unmatched depth of experience with the specific code requirements, home styles, and environmental conditions that define this region. From the Mediterranean-style estates of Rio Vista to the mid-century bungalows of Tarpon River, we've seen and handled it all.

Our A+ Better Business Bureau rating and 60+ years of combined team experience aren't just numbers — they reflect a genuine commitment to doing right by every homeowner we serve. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you get a dedicated project manager who knows Fort Lauderdale, knows the permit process, and handles every detail from initial inspection through final city sign-off. We pull all permits, coordinate all inspections, and never leave a project unfinished.

We also understand that a major home improvement investment should be accessible. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, so Fort Lauderdale homeowners in every zip code — from 33301 to 33334 — can prioritize their family's safety without straining their budget. Protecting your home shouldn't have to wait, and with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, it doesn't have to.

section_history.h2: Fort Lauderdale's Storm History Makes Impact Windows a Necessity, Not a Luxury
section_history.body:
Fort Lauderdale has weathered more than 10 named storms since 1990, and each one has left a mark on the city's understanding of what it means to be truly hurricane-ready. The most formative of these events was Hurricane Andrew in 1992, which devastated South Florida and exposed a catastrophic failure in residential building standards. While Andrew's worst damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade, Broward County felt its power — and the aftermath triggered a complete overhaul of Florida's building codes that ultimately gave rise to the HVHZ standards Fort Lauderdale homes must meet today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was perhaps Fort Lauderdale's most direct modern test. Wilma made landfall near Cape Romano and swept across the entire peninsula with sustained winds that hammered Broward County for hours, shattering windows, stripping roofs, and flooding homes throughout neighborhoods like Sailboat Bend, Victoria Park, and the areas around Las Olas Boulevard. Thousands of residents without impact-rated windows faced devastating interior damage from wind-driven rain and debris. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly over South Florida and served as yet another reminder that living this close to the Atlantic means living with the constant potential for life-altering storm damage.

The pattern is clear: Fort Lauderdale is not a city where homeowners can afford to gamble on standard windows. Each storm cycle reinforces the value of laminated, impact-rated glazing that keeps wind, water, and debris outside where they belong — protecting your family, your belongings, and the long-term structural integrity of your home. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs windows engineered specifically for this threat environment, giving Fort Lauderdale families the confidence to face hurricane season without fear.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Fort Lauderdale Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows
section_code.body:
Fort Lauderdale falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation shared only with Broward and Miami-Dade counties and representing the most stringent residential building code requirements in the United States. Under the Florida Building Code — which incorporates ASCE 7 wind load standards — every impact window installed in Fort Lauderdale must be tested and approved for a minimum design wind pressure appropriate to the 170 mph wind zone. Products must carry a valid Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval, demonstrating they've passed large and small missile impact testing as well as cyclic wind pressure testing. There are no exceptions and no shortcuts.

All impact window replacements in Fort Lauderdale require a permit through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division. This process involves submitting product approval documentation, installation drawings, and energy compliance data before any work begins. Inspections are required at key stages of the installation, and the final permit sign-off confirms that all work meets HVHZ standards. Attempting to install impact windows without a permit — a shortcut some less reputable contractors offer — can result in fines, forced removal, and significant complications when it comes time to sell or insure your home.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of the permitting process on your behalf. Our team is intimately familiar with the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division's requirements, submittal formats, and inspection timelines. We ensure that every product we install carries the proper NOA documentation and that all installation methods comply with the manufacturer's approved instructions — a requirement under HVHZ that is actively verified during inspections. When our crews finish a project in Fort Lauderdale, it's done right, done to code, and backed by every applicable warranty.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI: Premium Impact Window Brands Built for Fort Lauderdale
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines specifically chosen for their performance in South Florida's demanding coastal environment: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands carry the Miami-Dade NOA certifications required for HVHZ installation and are engineered to withstand the 170 mph wind loads that Fort Lauderdale's building code demands. These aren't generic box-store products — they're purpose-built, rigorously tested impact window systems designed for the exact climate and threat conditions that Fort Lauderdale homeowners face.

ESW Eastern Storm Windows are engineered with multi-chamber extruded aluminum frames that provide exceptional structural rigidity under wind pressure, paired with laminated safety glass featuring a tough PVB (polyvinyl butyral) interlayer. This interlayer is what keeps the glass intact upon impact — rather than shattering dangerously, the glass may crack but remains in the frame, preventing wind and water intrusion. ESW windows are also available with Low-E coatings that block up to 99% of UV radiation, dramatically reducing solar heat gain — a critical feature for Fort Lauderdale homes where air conditioning costs are a year-round concern. Low-E glass also protects interior furnishings, hardwood floors, and artwork from sun bleaching.

CWI impact windows offer a complementary product line with an emphasis on architectural versatility and thermal performance, making them an excellent choice for homes along Las Olas Boulevard and in the Victoria Park historic corridor where aesthetics matter alongside function. CWI's multi-chamber frame design enhances both structural performance and thermal insulation, reducing energy transfer through the frame itself. Together, ESW and CWI give Premier Impact Windows & Roofing the flexibility to match the right product to every home's unique combination of style, budget, and structural requirements — ensuring that every Fort Lauderdale homeowner gets the ideal solution for their specific situation.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost in Fort Lauderdale — and What's the Return on Investment?
section_cost.body:
For Fort Lauderdale homeowners, impact window replacement is one of the highest-value investments you can make in a property. With the average home value sitting at approximately $480,000, the financial case for upgrading to impact-rated windows is compelling from multiple angles: direct storm damage prevention, meaningful reductions in homeowner's insurance premiums, improved energy efficiency, and increased resale value. The upfront cost varies based on home size, window count, frame style, and product selection, but most Fort Lauderdale residential projects fall within a range that reflects the quality and compliance requirements of the HVHZ market.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we work hard to make that investment accessible. Our financing options start at just $87 per month with $0 down, allowing homeowners to begin protecting their families immediately rather than waiting until they've saved a lump sum. For a home in Sailboat Bend or Flagler Village with 15 to 20 windows, financing spreads the cost of a complete, code-compliant installation into a manageable monthly commitment — often one that is partially or fully offset by insurance savings alone. Many Fort Lauderdale homeowners are genuinely surprised to discover that their monthly insurance reduction nearly matches their financing payment.

Beyond insurance, the energy savings from Low-E laminated glass are substantial in a climate where air conditioning runs virtually year-round. Reduced UV and solar heat gain can lower cooling loads noticeably, delivering consistent utility bill savings month after month. And when it comes time to sell, impact windows are a significant selling point in the Fort Lauderdale market — buyers, real estate agents, and appraisers all recognize the value of a home that is fully hurricane-protected and code-compliant. In a competitive real estate market along Broward County's coast, that distinction can meaningfully impact both sale price and time on market.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Fort Lauderdale Homeowners Who Install Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially significant benefits of installing impact windows in Fort Lauderdale is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums — a benefit that is especially pronounced in Broward County, where windstorm insurance costs among the highest in the state. Florida's Insurance Code requires insurers to offer discounts for homes with verifiable wind mitigation features, and impact-rated windows on all openings represent one of the highest-value credits available under a Wind Mitigation Inspection Report. Many Fort Lauderdale homeowners see premium reductions of 20% to 45% after completing a full impact window installation and having a licensed wind mitigation inspector certify the work.

For homes insured through Citizens Property Insurance — Florida's state-backed insurer of last resort, which covers a significant share of Fort Lauderdale properties — the discounts for opening protection are codified and substantial. Private carriers including Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others operating in Broward County similarly offer meaningful credits for homes that achieve the highest level of opening protection under the OIR-B1-1802 wind mitigation form. To receive the full credit, all glazed openings including windows and glass doors must be protected with impact-rated products — partial upgrades do deliver savings, but full replacement maximizes the premium reduction.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides every customer with the documentation needed to support a wind mitigation inspection after installation, including product approval numbers, Miami-Dade NOA references, and installation verification. We recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector as soon as your project receives final permit approval from the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division — at that point, you can submit the completed OIR form to your insurance carrier and begin receiving your premium discount immediately. In many cases, the annual insurance savings alone represent a meaningful return on the total investment within just a few years of installation.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows if I'm not directly on Fort Lauderdale Beach?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Fort Lauderdale is entirely within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone regardless of your distance from the water. Whether you're in Flagler Village, Tarpon River, or Rio Vista, your home faces the same 170 mph wind zone designation and is subject to the same code requirements. Inland homes are still highly vulnerable to wind-borne debris and storm pressure — impact windows protect every property in the city, not just beachfront homes.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in Fort Lauderdale, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Project costs vary based on home size, window count, and product selection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible to upgrade your entire home without a large upfront payment. When you factor in insurance premium reductions — often 20% to 45% in Broward County — and energy savings from Low-E glass, many homeowners find their net monthly cost is significantly lower than the financing payment alone.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact window installation in Fort Lauderdale?
faqs.a: All impact window replacements in Fort Lauderdale require a permit through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division. This includes submitting Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval documentation, installation drawings, and energy compliance data. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting and inspections on your behalf — you never have to navigate the building department yourself. All our installations are fully permitted and pass final inspection before we consider a job complete.

faqs.q: How long does the impact window installation process take for a typical Fort Lauderdale home?
faqs.a: For a typical Fort Lauderdale single-family home, the physical installation usually takes one to three days depending on the number of windows and complexity of the openings. The overall timeline from contract signing to final permit approval typically runs three to six weeks, with the majority of that time attributed to permit processing at the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division. Our team keeps you informed at every stage and coordinates all inspections so the process is as smooth as possible.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact windows in Fort Lauderdale?
faqs.a: Fort Lauderdale homeowners who install impact-rated windows on all openings and complete a wind mitigation inspection commonly see premium reductions of 20% to 45%, depending on their carrier and current policy. Citizens Property Insurance and many private Broward County carriers offer some of the most significant opening protection credits available in Florida. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all documentation needed to support your wind mitigation inspection, helping you start saving as soon as your installation receives final permit approval.
